summary: Small matching PR that converted four previously incomplete functions to 100% matches: efAsync_8006737C in efasync and ft_80085004, ft_80085088, ft_800850B4 in ft_081B. The changes were mostly expression-shaping and type/temporary adjustments: efAsync_8006737C changed its argument type from s8 to int, flattened early returns, used arg_index directly, avoided local char* temporaries for fields of spC, and updated the header prototype. The fighter helpers introduced explicit co_attrs pointer temporaries and reordered declarations/loads to match compiler output. decomp.dev reported +340 matched bytes and 4 new matches; the PR was approved without written review comments.
what_changed: Changed efAsync_8006737C signature from void efAsync_8006737C(s8 arg_index) to void efAsync_8006737C(int arg_index) in both efasync.c and efasync.h.
In efAsync_8006737C, removed the separate int index local and used arg_index directly for bounds checks, array indexing intent, and psInitDataBankLoad/psInitDataBank calls.
Restructured efAsync_8006737C control flow from a nested if (index >= 0) block into explicit early returns for out-of-range indices, NULL ef_DAT_file, and existing unk8.
Changed the bounds check in efAsync_8006737C to reject both arg_index >= 50 and arg_index < 0.
In efAsync_8006737C, removed local char* DAT_filename and effDateTable_name temporaries and referenced spC->ef_DAT_file and spC->effDataTable_name directly in the bitwise OR check and databank calls.
Kept temp_r31->unk8 assignment after the optional psInitDataBankLoad/psInitDataBank initialization, assigning it to (void*) ((u8*) spC + 8).
In ft_80085004 and ft_80085088, split declaration and assignment of ftCo_DatAttrs* co_attrs so Fighter* fp is declared first, then co_attrs is assigned from &fp->co_attrs.
In ft_800850B4, replaced a separate float dir temporary and direct fp->co_attrs.gr_friction access with a co_attrs pointer temporary and a direct fp->facing_dir1 argument.
smart_moves: Synchronized the efAsync_8006737C prototype in the header with the source change, avoiding a source/header type mismatch after changing s8 to int.
Used direct struct field access on spC instead of naming intermediate char* locals, which likely helped preserve the compiler's load/use pattern for the 100% efasync match.
Flattened nested guard logic in efAsync_8006737C into early returns, a common matching tactic when the target assembly branches out before the main work block.
For the fighter helpers, introduced co_attrs pointer temporaries even where the old C was semantically equivalent; this is a precise decomp tactic for matching register allocation and load ordering.
Removed the float dir temporary in ft_800850B4 and passed fp->facing_dir1 directly, while routing gr_friction through co_attrs, matching three closely related helper functions consistently.
decomp_lessons: Function argument signedness/width can be decisive for matching; here efAsync_8006737C needed int rather than s8, and the header had to be updated with it.
Semantically harmless locals can hurt matching. Removing DAT_filename, effDateTable_name, index, and dir changed codegen enough to produce full matches.
Declaration order and split initialization matter in this codebase's compiler output: declaring a pointer first and assigning it after GET_FIGHTER can recover small one-byte match gaps.
When a function does range validation, matching may require the exact guard shape, including explicit negative checks and early returns instead of wrapping the body in an if.
For repeated helper wrappers around Fighter data, prefer matching the observed load pattern over compact source: GET_FIGHTER, take &fp->co_attrs into ftCo_DatAttrs*, then pass specific fields.
naming_conventions: Effect async symbols use the efAsync_800xxxxx address-based prefix style, such as efAsync_8006737C.
Fighter helper symbols in this area use ft_800xxxxx address-based names, such as ft_80085004, ft_80085088, and ft_800850B4.
Fighter data pointer locals follow established short names like fp for Fighter* and co_attrs for ftCo_DatAttrs*.
The PR retained placeholder/unknown names such as unk8 and _struct_efAsync_803C025C_0xC; there is no evidence of new semantic renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Change parameter type s8 to int to avoid unwanted sign-extension/truncation behavior and align the function prototype with assembly expectations.
Use early-return guard clauses to match branch layout instead of nested conditionals.
Avoid unnecessary alias locals for struct fields when the target code appears to load directly from the struct pointer.
Use bitwise OR on cast pointer values, (u32) spC->ef_DAT_file | (u32) spC->effDataTable_name, to preserve the observed combined non-null check shape.
Split local declaration from assignment in small wrapper functions to influence register allocation and instruction order.
Route repeated struct field reads through a pointer temporary, such as co_attrs = &fp->co_attrs, when direct member access does not match.
review_feedback: No written review comments were present in the slice.
ribbanya approved the PR without a review body.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code improved to 52.98% (+0.01%, +340 bytes) with 4 new matches.
Reported new matches: efAsync_8006737C reached 100.00% from 93.27%, and ft_80085004, ft_80085088, ft_800850B4 each reached 100.00% from 98.64%.
follow_up_queries: Find other efAsync functions where s8 parameters may actually need int for matching and header consistency.
Search ft_081B.c for small wrapper functions around GET_FIGHTER and co_attrs that may need split declaration/assignment patterns.
Look for remaining incomplete functions with one-byte or very small mismatches caused by unnecessary temporaries or declaration order.
Audit callers of efAsync_8006737C to confirm int argument use is consistent with the updated prototype.
